2606.31508.title: Building an ASR Solution for Training and Assessing Children's Reading
2606.31508.paper_summary: Automatic speech recognition for children's reading remains underdeveloped for most African languages, including Bambara, despite its potential value for reproducible literacy assessment. We present an open-source system for assessing children's reading in Bambara, developed through an end-to-end process linking field data collection, benchmark construction, model adaptation, a reading application, and classroom validation. A mobile collection and assessment app was used to collect 55 hours of raw reading speech from 60 children, from which we construct a public benchmark for Bambara child-reading assessment. Fine-tuning experiments compare Soloni, a Bambara-adapted Fast-Conformer ASR framework with TDT and CTC decoders, with QuartzNet, a compact convolutional ASR architecture. The best Soloni model reduces WER from 0.42 to 0.22 and CER from 0.15 to 0.08, substantially outperforming QuartzNet on the isolated benchmark. The experiments further show that repeated readings of the same texts provide architecture-dependent benefits: they substantially improve QuartzNet but add only marginal gains for Soloni, while SpecAugment regulates training without exceeding the best unaugmented configuration. Disaggregated analysis identifies children under 10 as the main source of residual errors, motivating targeted collection from younger readers. Ten classroom trials supported continued use of the application.
2606.31508.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音識別在兒童閱讀方面對於大多數非洲語言仍然發展不足，包括班巴拉語，儘管它對於可重複的識字評估具有潛在價值。我們提出了一個開源系統，用於評估班巴拉語兒童的閱讀，該系統通過一個端到端的過程開發，連接了現場數據收集、基準建設、模型適應、閱讀應用程序和課堂驗證。一個移動收集和評估應用程序被用來收集60名兒童的55小時原始閱讀語音，從中我們構建了一個班巴拉語兒童閱讀評估的公共基準。微調實驗比較了Soloni，一個班巴拉語適應的Fast-Conformer ASR框架，配備TDT和CTC解碼器，與QuartzNet，一個緊湊的卷積ASR架構。最佳的Soloni模型將WER從0.42降低到0.22，CER從0.15降低到0.08，顯著超越了QuartzNet在孤立基準上的表現。實驗進一步顯示，相同文本的重複閱讀提供了依賴於架構的好處：它們顯著改善了QuartzNet，但對Soloni僅帶來邊際增益，而SpecAugment則在不超過最佳未增強配置的情況下調節訓練。分解分析確定10歲以下的兒童是剩餘錯誤的主要來源，促使針對年輕讀者的定向收集。十次課堂試驗支持該應用程序的持續使用。
2606.31508.author: Yacouba Diarra et.al.
2606.31508.authors: Yacouba Diarra, Nouhoum Souleymane Coulibaly, Mamadou Dembele, Aymane Dembele, Michael Leventhal
